description: Accessing shared mutable state from multiple threads without synchronization, producing a data race and undefined behavior.
body: Failure mode: two threads read/write the same non-atomic variable or container without a mutex or atomic, so the program has a data race; results are nondeterministic and the optimizer may assume no race. Detection signals: ThreadSanitizer reports a data race with both stacks; values are occasionally wrong under load; bugs vanish under a debugger (timing). Do not share mutable state across threads unprotected. Safer fallback: guard the state with std::mutex/scoped_lock or use std::atomic for simple counters, prefer message passing where possible, and confirm a clean `-fsanitize=thread` run under load.
